name: api.video
what_it_does: API-first video hosting and streaming platform that handles video upload, encoding, CDN delivery, and live streaming via a developer-friendly REST API.
use_cases: Upload and host videos with automatic adaptive bitrate encoding
Deliver videos via global CDN with embeddable player
Build live streaming features into applications
Add video capabilities to SaaS products or mobile apps
Replace expensive video infrastructure with managed API service
not_for: Large-scale broadcast or linear TV workflows — use Brightcove or AWS Elemental
Teams that need an end-to-end CMS with editorial tools — this is infrastructure, not a platform
Very high-volume production workloads on the free tier (30 min/month limit)
best_when: You are a developer or product team that needs to add video hosting, playback, or live streaming to an application without managing encoding infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need advanced DRM, complex ad insertion workflows, or broadcast-grade SLAs with dedicated support.

auth.notes: API key for server-to-server; OAuth 2.0 client credentials for delegated access. Sandbox environment available with separate API key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 30 minutes of uploaded video per month; watermarked player
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go based on encoding minutes and bandwidth; starts around $49/month for small teams
pricing.notes: Transparent pricing page; free sandbox environment for development. Storage and delivery charged separately.

performance.rate_limits: 300 requests/minute on paid plans; lower on free tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured JSON error responses with problem codes and human-readable messages; good for agent parsing
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Video uploads support resumable upload protocol; most write operations are not idempotent by default
known_agent_gotchas: Large video uploads require chunked/resumable upload — single POST will fail for big files
Free tier watermarks player; agents building production flows need paid plan
Encoding is asynchronous — agents must poll or use webhooks for completion status
Live stream keys are separate from VOD API keys — different auth flow
Sandbox and production environments use different API keys; easy to mix up

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, API key + OAuth 2.0, GDPR compliant, private video support with tokens
